transcript.clean_text: the welter it never disappoints even if the weather is rather challenging sometimes and so it was shrouding jay vines victory but his joy just blew away the clouds fabulous victory for him and a great second place for ramco ever nepal as he went into the red jersey he and then rick mouse coming in together and indeed putting some of the main rivals much further behind pretty much roglich a minute and one off ever nepal our leader he sits 21 seconds in front of rudy monarch and enric mass so what would stage seven offer to us well a strange course and one that would pose a few questions rolling terrain early on then a mighty climb dead center and then a gradually descending road to a finish how would it be played well it begged a breakaway and all talk was of that at the front of the pack before we started in the end six riders got up the road jesus aurora for coffee this ballistella from astana we had fred wright again from bahrain victorious omar goldstein of israel premier tech harry sweeney from lodi sedal and jimmy hansen's from alps and de kerning this is what they were dealing with divided into three parts the main section being that dreadful climb how would it be taken and what would the gap be well these were the six riders that managed to escape took a while with oscar tell us cody forming the main chase but they had to let them go the gap went up to about four minutes and 20 at one point and then the hammer was thrown down by trek sagafredo on the big climb of the day the idea was to put sprinters in trouble merlier was off the back so indeed was this man akama sam bennett seemed to be struggling but his teammates made sure that he stayed in touch so they failed to shake off some of the main rivals to matt's petters up front jesus harada crested the day's only climb trek still punishing but sustaining those blows was the man in green well would it be a battle plan failure for trek segafredo when we came over the top of the mountain we came into pleasant weather and the high speed descent 50 kilometers from home it seemed that a gap of 2 minutes and 28 might well be bridged but the tailwind was set fair for the break and it didn't matter even though trek segafredo took their foot off the gas up front others came through bike exchange archaea samsic working of course for caden groves and dan mcclay it was all ultimately to no avail high pace through the day and finally the realization that this breakaway was going to make it home a gap of a minute with 10 k's to go sprinters teams eased up thinking about minor points perhaps it was all down to the breakaway well after such pace throughout the day who would have anything left well first man to test the water was jimmy hansen's for ups into kernick and then over the final rise down to the line fred wright went early harry sweeney just couldn't respond going to the center road jesus harada going the other side to the barriers was battistella it was between those two as fed was ultimately destined to finish third harada on the throw beating batista hugely emotional moment for jesus harada the pack came through a few moments later it was sam bennett that crossed the line first adding to his points advantage over matt pedersen whose team had done so much harada fell into the arms of his teammates second spanish victory and the overhead shot shows that he timed it absolutely perfectly the fash finishing samuel batistella simply ran out of rope harada taking it ahead of busester fred wright in third place he's having a great tour janssen's sweeney bennett stewart getting up groves palace and finishing ninth after all that hard work dan mcclay rounding out the top ten blemco eva nepal he'll be well hoping that this weekend which is incredibly difficult we'll still see him in red by the end of it he leads molar by 21 seconds henrik massey in third place roglic a minute and one down sam bennett enhances his position in the green jersey but must look at the parkour this coming weekend with fear as all the fast men will he's on 142 points that's pedestal his nearest rival on 127. and this is what saturday brings hell on earth if you don't like climbing if you do it could be heaven climbing straight out of the back door and the day never stops going either up or down virtually no flat on a punishing parkour and pretty much more of the same on sunday we cannot wait for the action we'll be seeing it from flag to line don't miss it
